parameters.text:
=# SYSTEM PROMPT: KRAKOW REAL ESTATE ANALYSIS EXPERT ## ROLE You are a precise real estate market analyst. Your task is to extract technical data from the listing HTML code and assign a rating (ai_rating) based on defined user preferences. ## YOUR PREFERENCES (RATING CRITERIA) Assign `ai_rating` on a 1-10 scale, where 10 is the highest rating, considering: - **Location:**: Krakow (highest score: South Krakow). - **Area**: minimum 90 sqm. - **Layout:**: 3 bedrooms and 2 bathrooms, terrace. - **Amenities**: elevator. - **Negatives (lower the rating):**: electric heating, gas. - **Building type**: boutique (under 50 apartments). ## HINT FROM PAST USER FEEDBACK Use the following past user decisions as a soft hint only. Prioritize strong signals first: grade 5 over 4, and grade 1 over 2. Prefer current listing facts over pattern matching. {{ $json.feedback_hint }} ## INPUT DATA - **EXTERNAL_ID**: {{ $json.external_id }} - **Title**: {{ $json.title }} - **Input price**: {{ $json.price }} PLN - **Input price per m2**: {{ $json.price_per_m2 }} PLN - **Area**: {{ $json.area }} m2 - **Lot size**: {{ $json.lot_size }} m2 - **Construction year**: {{ $json.construction_year }} ## LISTING PAGE CONTENT (FOR ANALYSIS)##: {{ $json.raw_html || '' }} ## DATA EXTRACTION INSTRUCTIONS Analyse the provided HTML inside and find and fill in gaps in the input data: 1. **title**: Full, attractive listing title. 2. **price**: Total property price (remove "PLN", "PLN", spaces). Return as a number. 3. **price_per_m2**: Price per sqm. If not available, calculate: price / area. 4. **area**: Apartment area (number). 5. **lot_size**: Lot size in ares (only for houses, otherwise 0). 6. **year**: Construction year (if missing, estimate or use 0). 7. **ai_rating**: Your rating (1-10) based on the above preferences. ## EMAIL SUMMARY PREPARATION INSTRUCTIONS (email_html_content field) Generate HTML content to be inserted into the email summary section. Focus on substantive analysis. HTML content requirements: AI Verdict: Brief justification of the ai_rating score (np. "Ideal area and location, but deducting points for gas"). Pros Analysis: List features matching preferences (np. southern location, terrace, boutique building, 2 bathrooms). Risk Analysis (Cons): Clearly indicate what lowered the rating (np. "gas heating", "no elevator", "no information about number of apartments in the building"). Location: Provide the most precise location possible (district, street). Styling: Use clean HTML. Use ,
    ,
  • . Do not add CSS styles inside this field (they will be overridden by the n8n template). Example structure inside the string: "Listing analysis: The apartment meets the key area requirement (90m2+) and has the desired 3-bedroom layout.
    • ✅ South Krakow (Debniki)
    • ✅ Terrace and 2 bathrooms
    • ❌ Gas heating (rating lowered)
    " ## OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT JSON) Return ONLY a clean JSON object. Do not add any comments or introductions. Under no circumstances modify the EXTERNAL_ID value = {{ $json.external_id }} EXTERNAL_ID is in text format, type "string" { "external_id": "string", "title": "string", "price": number, "price_per_m2": number, "area": number, "lot_size": number, "construction_year": number, "ai_rating": number, "ai_analysis_html": "string" }

parameters.content:
## Real Estate AI Agent

**Version:** `1.0.1`
**Git SHA:** `5a08417`
**Built:** `2026-04-28`

Upload check:
- workflow name is `Real Estate AI Agent`
- metadata node is `Workflow Metadata - v1.0.1`

parameters.query:
SELECT * FROM rea_property_offers
WHERE COALESCE(is_in_trash, FALSE) = FALSE
  AND (
    ai_rating IS NULL
    OR ai_rating < 1
    OR ai_rating > 10
    OR COALESCE(NULLIF(ai_analysis_html, ''), '') = ''
    OR NULLIF(BTRIM(COALESCE(title, '')), '') IS NULL
    OR COALESCE(price, 0) <= 0
    OR COALESCE(price_per_m2, 0) <= 0
    OR COALESCE(area, 0) <= 0
    OR COALESCE(construction_year, 0) <= 0
  )
  AND NULLIF(BTRIM(COALESCE(external_id, '')), '') IS NOT NULL
  AND LOWER(BTRIM(COALESCE(external_id, ''))) <> 'undefined'
  AND NULLIF(BTRIM(COALESCE(url, '')), '') IS NOT NULL
  AND LOWER(BTRIM(COALESCE(url, ''))) <> 'undefined'
  AND BTRIM(url) ~* '^https?://'
ORDER BY created_at ASC;

parameters.query:
WITH incoming AS (
    SELECT
        '{{ $json.external_id }}'::text AS external_id,
        '{{ $json.category || 'mieszkanie-krakow' }}'::text AS category,
        '{{ $json.url }}'::text AS url,
        '{{ String($json.title || '').replace(/'/g, "''") }}'::text AS title,
        {{ Number($json.price) || 0 }}::numeric AS price,
        {{ Number($json.price_per_m2) || 0 }}::numeric AS price_per_m2,
        {{ Number($json.area) || 0 }}::numeric AS area,
        {{ Number($json.lot_size) || 0 }}::numeric AS lot_size,
        {{ Number($json.construction_year) || 0 }}::integer AS construction_year,
        '{{ $json.created_at || new Date().toISOString() }}'::timestamp AS created_at,
        NOW()::timestamp AS last_seen_at
)
INSERT INTO rea_property_offers (
    external_id, category, url, title,
    price, price_per_m2, area, lot_size,
    construction_year, ai_rating, user_rating,
    created_at, last_seen_at
)
SELECT
    external_id, category, url, title,
    price, price_per_m2, area, lot_size,
    construction_year, NULL, 'pending',
    created_at, last_seen_at
FROM incoming
WHERE
    NULLIF(BTRIM(external_id), '') IS NOT NULL
    AND LOWER(BTRIM(external_id)) <> 'undefined'
    AND NULLIF(BTRIM(url), '') IS NOT NULL
    AND LOWER(BTRIM(url)) <> 'undefined'
    AND BTRIM(url) ~* '^https?://'
ON CONFLICT (external_id) DO UPDATE SET
    category = EXCLUDED.category,
    url = EXCLUDED.url,
    title = COALESCE(NULLIF(EXCLUDED.title, ''), rea_property_offers.title),
    price = CASE WHEN COALESCE(EXCLUDED.price, 0) > 0 THEN EXCLUDED.price ELSE rea_property_offers.price END,
    price_per_m2 = CASE WHEN COALESCE(EXCLUDED.price_per_m2, 0) > 0 THEN EXCLUDED.price_per_m2 ELSE rea_property_offers.price_per_m2 END,
    area = CASE WHEN COALESCE(EXCLUDED.area, 0) > 0 THEN EXCLUDED.area ELSE rea_property_offers.area END,
    lot_size = CASE WHEN COALESCE(EXCLUDED.lot_size, 0) > 0 THEN EXCLUDED.lot_size ELSE rea_property_offers.lot_size END,
    construction_year = CASE WHEN COALESCE(EXCLUDED.construction_year, 0) > 0 THEN EXCLUDED.construction_year ELSE rea_property_offers.construction_year END,
    last_seen_at = EXCLUDED.last_seen_at
RETURNING *;

parameters.query:
SELECT * FROM rea_property_offers
WHERE sent_at IS NULL
  AND COALESCE(is_in_trash, FALSE) = FALSE
  AND COALESCE(excluded_from_feedback_loop, FALSE) = FALSE
  AND ai_rating BETWEEN 4 AND 10
  AND COALESCE(NULLIF(ai_analysis_html, ''), '') <> ''
ORDER BY ai_rating DESC, created_at DESC;

parameters.subject: ={{ $json.title }}
parameters.message:
=

{{ $json.title }}

💰 Price: {{ $json.price.toLocaleString() }} PLN ({{ $json.price_per_m2 }} PLN/m2)

{{ $json.ai_analysis_html }}

Your verdict:

👍 I like it 👎 Not for me

🔗 View original listing

parameters.query:
WITH rated_offers AS (
    SELECT
        external_id,
        title,
        user_notes,
        user_rated_at,
        COALESCE(
            user_grade,
            CASE user_rating
                WHEN 'like' THEN 4
                WHEN '👍 I like it' THEN 4
                WHEN 'dislike' THEN 2
                WHEN '👎 I don''t like it' THEN 2
                ELSE NULL
            END
        ) AS effective_grade
    FROM rea_property_offers
    WHERE COALESCE(is_in_trash, FALSE) = FALSE
      AND COALESCE(excluded_from_feedback_loop, FALSE) = FALSE
),
best_examples AS (
    SELECT *
    FROM rated_offers
    WHERE effective_grade IN (5, 4)
    ORDER BY CASE effective_grade WHEN 5 THEN 0 WHEN 4 THEN 1 ELSE 2 END, user_rated_at DESC NULLS LAST, external_id
    LIMIT 3
),
worst_examples AS (
    SELECT *
    FROM rated_offers
    WHERE effective_grade IN (1, 2)
    ORDER BY CASE effective_grade WHEN 1 THEN 0 WHEN 2 THEN 1 ELSE 2 END, user_rated_at DESC NULLS LAST, external_id
    LIMIT 3
)
SELECT CONCAT(
    'BEST RATED EXAMPLES (prioritize grade 5, then 4; up to 3):',
    COALESCE(
        (
            SELECT E'\n' || string_agg(
                format(
                    '- grade=%s | external_id=%s | title=%s | notes=%s',
                    effective_grade,
                    external_id,
                    COALESCE(left(title, 140), 'n/a'),
                    COALESCE(NULLIF(left(user_notes, 200), ''), 'none')
                ),
                E'\n'
            )
            FROM best_examples
        ),
        E'\n- none yet'
    ),
    E'\n\nWORST RATED EXAMPLES (prioritize grade 1, then 2; up to 3):',
    COALESCE(
        (
            SELECT E'\n' || string_agg(
                format(
                    '- grade=%s | external_id=%s | title=%s | notes=%s',
                    effective_grade,
                    external_id,
                    COALESCE(left(title, 140), 'n/a'),
                    COALESCE(NULLIF(left(user_notes, 200), ''), 'none')
                ),
                E'\n'
            )
            FROM worst_examples
        ),
        E'\n- none yet'
    )
) AS feedback_hint;

json.title: Kraków – Kazimierz – ul. Szeroka - 90 m²
json.ai_analysis_html:
Listing analysis: This property is located in a prime district of Krakow and meets the minimum area requirement. However, it receives deductions for not fully matching the preferred layout, lacking an elevator, and requiring renovation.
AI Verdict: Good area and flexible layout in a prime Krakow location, but deductions for specific layout features, lack of an elevator, and the need for renovation.
Pros Analysis:

  • ✅ Location: Highly desirable Kazimierz district in Krakow.

  • ✅ Area: Meets the minimum requirement of 90 sqm.

  • ✅ Layout: Dwupoziomowe (two-level) with 4 rooms, adaptable to achieve 3 bedrooms.

  • ✅ Heating: District heating (miejskie), avoiding electric or gas heating.

  • ✅ Building type: Kamienica (tenement house), likely fitting the boutique building type criteria.


Risk Analysis (Cons):

  • ❌ Layout: Only 1 bathroom available, while 2 bathrooms are preferred.

  • ❌ Layout: Features a balcony, not a terrace as preferred.

  • ❌ Amenities: No elevator in the building.

  • ❌ Condition: The apartment is described as "Do remontu" (requires renovation).


Location: Kraków, Kazimierz, ul. Szeroka.
